frameworks.name: adaptive gain control
frameworks.one_line: single normalization mechanism unifying distraction effects across decision domains

frameworks.name: perceptual decision making
frameworks.one_line: tilt illusion reframed as gain modulation by irrelevant context

frameworks.name: cognitive control / conflict adaptation
frameworks.one_line: Eriksen flanker conflict effects predicted by gain-controlled decision variable

frameworks.name: economic decision making
frameworks.one_line: decoy effects in value-guided choice explained by adaptive gain

core_question: Can a single adaptive gain control model account for distraction effects across perceptual, cognitive, and value-guided choice?
key_assumptions: irrelevant information modulates the gain of decision-relevant signals
the same gain mechanism operates across domains
a dorsal cortical network indexes the gain-modulated decision variable
manipulations: presence and type of distractors in flanker variants
key_findings: adaptive gain control accounts for tilt illusion, conflict adaptation, and decoy effects
model predicts counterintuitive new behavioral phenomena in flanker task variants
BOLD signals in a dorsal network including anterior cingulate cortex track the gain-modulated decision variable
interpretations: distraction across domains reflects a unified adaptive gain mechanism
anterior cingulate cortex implements gain modulation of decision signals
